flashcards.q: What do we technically call the original substances that undergo a chemical change?
What do we correctly call the new substances fundamentally formed during a chemical reaction?
What mathematically constitutes a skeletal chemical equation?
flashcards.a: An unbalanced chemical equation that only lists the formulas of reactants and products without definitively equalizing the specific atom counts on both sides.

flashcards.q: Why strictly must all chemical equations be accurately and completely balanced?
flashcards.a: To physically and universally satisfy the Law of Conservation of Mass.

flashcards.q: What specific type of reaction cleanly forms a single unique product directly from two or more active reactants?
Which fundamental type of reaction structurally requires an active input of external energy (like heat, light, or electricity) to successfully proceed?
flashcards.a: A decomposition reaction (which operates inherently as an endothermic reaction).

flashcards.q: What identically classifies a reaction specifically as an exothermic reaction?
flashcards.a: It is a chemical reaction that naturally releases active thermal heat energy outward alongside the physical formation of its designated products.

flashcards.q: In which specific reaction does a chemically more reactive element independently replace a significantly less reactive element permanently within its compound?
What explicitly defines a generic precipitation reaction?
flashcards.a: Any double displacement exchange reaction that structurally visibly produces a solid, completely insoluble salt formally known as a precipitate.

flashcards.q: What unequivocally occurs structurally when a substance is correctly fully oxidized?
flashcards.a: It chemically gains an attached oxygen atom, or actively structurally loses a bonded hydrogen atom.

flashcards.q: What cleanly characterizes a full verified redox reaction?
flashcards.a: It is exactly a paired chemical reaction where active oxidation and reliable reduction consistently happen simultaneously.
The gradual natural physical degradation of active metals slowly occurring when they precisely interact destructively with environmental elements, exactly like trapped moisture and ambient oxygen.

flashcards.q: Why do manufacturers heavily flush commercial bags of potato chips securely with nitrogen gas?
flashcards.a: To safely isolate the chips blocking active oxygen, effectively cleanly preventing the natural oxidation (rancidity) of the delicate cooking fats and oils inside.
